content.content:
1	# GSD-T Progress
2	
3	## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
4	## Status: M44 IN PROGRESS — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)
5	## Date: 2026-04-22
6	## Version: 3.17.10
7	
8	## Current Milestone
9	
10	**M44 Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)** — IN PROGRESS (7 proposed domains, scoped in backlog #14). Target: v3.18.10.
11	
12	**Goal**: Deliver task-level parallelism to **both** execution modes on equal footing, with mode-aware gating math. Unattended mode adds the harder contract: **zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run**.
13	
14	**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
15	- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt.
16	- **[unattended]** Run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and **zero compaction**. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate.
17	
18	**Three pre-existing invariants (apply to BOTH modes)**: file-disjointness proof BEFORE spawn · 100% automatic merges · pre-spawn economics check.
19	
20	**Two delivery layers**: L1 parallel `claude -p` worker spawns (primary lever for both modes) · L2 parallel tasks within one worker (weaker lever, when L1 isn't economic).
21	
22	**Pre-reqs landed**: Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`) · Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8`/`f7de324`) · Q2b compact_marker frame + visualizer badge (`8abe4ef`) · Q3 turn→tool join fix (`8f4588b`) · optimization report generator (`b5edff2`) · adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`).
23	
24	**Calibration corpus**: 525 turn rows (`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`) + 72 compaction events (`.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`) + per-CW report (`.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md`).
25	
26	**Proposed domains (7)** — full scope in `.gsd-t/backlog.md` #14:
27	- D1 — Generic task-graph reader (parses domain tasks + cross-domain deps → DAG)
28	- D2 — `gsd-t parallel` CLI (subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level parallelism, `--mode` aware)
29	- D3 — Command-file integration (`execute`/`wave`/`quick`/`debug`/`integrate` consume task-graph)
30	- D4 — Dep-graph validation (refuses fan-out when deps unmet)
31	- D5 — File-disjointness prover (walks expected-touch lists, sequential fallback if unprovable)
32	- D6 — Pre-spawn economics + cost estimator (queries token-usage.jsonl for similar-task cost, mode-aware gating)
33	- D7 — Per-CW token attribution + compaction-event integration (`cw_id` tagging + estimator calibration loop)
34	
35	**Success criteria**:
36	- [in-session] `gsd-t-execute` previously taking T min serially completes in ≤ T/2 min with N workers, zero pause/resume prompts
37	- [unattended] M1 → M5 minimum (ideally M1 → M10) runs end-to-end with **zero compaction events** during the autonomous chain
38	
39	**Recommended flow**: `partition` → `discuss` (D1 task-graph schema + D6 estimator algorithm need design) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10.
40	
41	**Previous milestone (M43)** completed 2026-04-21, v3.17.10 tagged. Reference DEFINED block preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/` archive.
42	
43	**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.
44	
45	**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.
46	
47	**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
48	- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
49	- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
50	- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).
51	
52	**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
53	- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
54	- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
55	- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.
56	
57	**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.
58	
59	**Out of scope (deferred)**:
60	- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
61	- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
62	- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.
63	
64	**Contracts touched**:
65	- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
66	- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
67	- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
68	- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)
69	
70	**Success criteria**:
71	1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
72	2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
73	3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
74	4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
75	5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
76	6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.
77	
78	**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.
79	
80	**Previous milestone (M42) retained for reference below** (M42 Live Spawn Transcript Viewer — COMPLETE, v3.16.10 tagged 2026-04-20, 3 domains, 29 M42 tests, 1522/1522 suite).

# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M44 IN PROGRESS — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)
## Date: 2026-04-22
## Version: 3.17.10

## Current Milestone

**M44 Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)** — IN PROGRESS (7 proposed domains, scoped in backlog #14). Target: v3.18.10.

**Goal**: Deliver task-level parallelism to **both** execution modes on equal footing, with mode-aware gating math. Unattended mode adds the harder contract: **zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run**.

**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt.
- **[unattended]** Run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and **zero compaction**. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate.

**Three pre-existing invariants (apply to BOTH modes)**: file-disjointness proof BEFORE spawn · 100% automatic merges · pre-spawn economics check.

**Two delivery layers**: L1 parallel `claude -p` worker spawns (primary lever for both modes) · L2 parallel tasks within one worker (weaker lever, when L1 isn't economic).

**Pre-reqs landed**: Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`) · Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8`/`f7de324`) · Q2b compact_marker frame + visualizer badge (`8abe4ef`) · Q3 turn→tool join fix (`8f4588b`) · optimization report generator (`b5edff2`) · adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`).

**Calibration corpus**: 525 turn rows (`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`) + 72 compaction events (`.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`) + per-CW report (`.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md`).

**Proposed domains (7)** — full scope in `.gsd-t/backlog.md` #14:
- D1 — Generic task-graph reader (parses domain tasks + cross-domain deps → DAG)
- D2 — `gsd-t parallel` CLI (subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level parallelism, `--mode` aware)
- D3 — Command-file integration (`execute`/`wave`/`quick`/`debug`/`integrate` consume task-graph)
- D4 — Dep-graph validation (refuses fan-out when deps unmet)
- D5 — File-disjointness prover (walks expected-touch lists, sequential fallback if unprovable)
- D6 — Pre-spawn economics + cost estimator (queries token-usage.jsonl for similar-task cost, mode-aware gating)
- D7 — Per-CW token attribution + compaction-event integration (`cw_id` tagging + estimator calibration loop)

**Success criteria**:
- [in-session] `gsd-t-execute` previously taking T min serially completes in ≤ T/2 min with N workers, zero pause/resume prompts
- [unattended] M1 → M5 minimum (ideally M1 → M10) runs end-to-end with **zero compaction events** during the autonomous chain

**Recommended flow**: `partition` → `discuss` (D1 task-graph schema + D6 estimator algorithm need design) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10.

**Previous milestone (M43)** completed 2026-04-21, v3.17.10 tagged. Reference DEFINED block preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/` archive.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

**Previous milestone (M42) retained for reference below** (M42 Live Spawn Transcript Viewer — COMPLETE, v3.16.10 tagged 2026-04-20, 3 domains, 29 M42 tests, 1522/1522 suite).

1	# M44 Partition — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)
2	
3	**Status**: PARTITIONED
4	**Date**: 2026-04-22
5	**Target version**: 3.18.10
6	**Domains**: 7 (D1 task-graph-reader · D2 parallel-cli · D3 command-file-integration · D4 depgraph-validation · D5 file-disjointness-prover · D6 pre-spawn-economics · D7 per-cw-attribution)
7	**Waves**: 3 (Foundation → Gates → Integration)
8	**Rationale source**: M44 scope 2026-04-22 (mode-aware parallelism; see `.gsd-t/progress.md` M44 "Current Milestone" + backlog #14).
9	
10	## Theme Split
11	
12	- **Layer 1 — Parallel `claude -p` worker spawns**: primary lever for both modes. K workers each with a clean CW. For [unattended], this is the compaction-elimination mechanism — slicing work into smaller per-worker pieces that never approach the CW ceiling. For [in-session], it is primarily a wall-clock reducer. D2 delivers the CLI wrapping M40 orchestrator; D3 wires it into the command files.
13	- **Layer 2 — Parallel tasks within one worker**: weaker lever, bounded by one CW. Used when L1 isn't economic (small tasks, shared context). Same disjointness/economics gates apply at sub-iter granularity. This layer is part of D2's `--mode in-session` path.
14	
15	**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
16	- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt. Silent compaction is acceptable; demanding user attention is not.
17	- **[unattended]** Zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate. Speed is a side-benefit. Supervisor (Node, no CW) orchestrates `claude -p` workers.
18	
19	## Domains
20	
21	### D1 — m44-d1-task-graph-reader
22	**Responsibility**: parse `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md` and cross-domain dependency declarations; emit a validated DAG of independently-executable task slices. Mode-agnostic.
23	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d1-task-graph-reader/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
24	
25	### D2 — m44-d2-parallel-cli
26	**Responsibility**: new `gsd-t parallel` subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level (not just domain-level) parallelism. Honors `--mode in-session|unattended` flag (or auto-detects from caller). Both modes. Consumes D1 DAG, D4 dep-gate, D5 disjointness-gate, D6 economics gate.
27	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d2-parallel-cli/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
28	
29	### D3 — m44-d3-command-file-integration
30	**Responsibility**: `gsd-t-execute`, `gsd-t-wave`, `gsd-t-quick`, `gsd-t-debug`, `gsd-t-integrate` learn to consume the task-graph via D2 CLI and dispatch parallel workers. Both modes. Depends on D2's stable CLI surface.
31	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d3-command-file-integration/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
32	
33	### D4 — m44-d4-depgraph-validation
34	**Responsibility**: pre-spawn validator that confirms task dependencies are honored before fan-out; refuses to spawn tasks whose dependencies have not completed. Mode-agnostic. Read-only against task state artifacts.
35	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d4-depgraph-validation/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
36	
37	### D5 — m44-d5-file-disjointness-prover
38	**Responsibility**: walk each task's expected-touch list against every other concurrent task's list; prove no shared write targets before parallel spawn. Falls back to sequential when unprovable. Sources: domain `scope.md`, prior commit history for similar tasks, optional explicit `touches:` field on task stubs. Mode-agnostic.
39	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d5-file-disjointness-prover/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
40	
41	### D6 — m44-d6-pre-spawn-economics
42	**Responsibility**: query `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` for prior-similar-task token cost (matches by command + step + domain); estimate per-worker CW footprint; decide parallel-vs-sequential. Mode-aware: feeds [in-session] orchestrator-CW gate vs [unattended] per-worker-CW gate. Calibrated against the 525-row + 72-event corpus.
43	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d6-pre-spawn-economics/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
44	
45	### D7 — m44-d7-per-cw-attribution
46	**Responsibility**: ensure every spawn (parallel or sequential) tags its token-usage rows with `cw_id` so the per-CW rollup keeps working post-M44. Wire the existing `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` hook output into the supervisor's "we failed to prevent compaction" signal for [unattended] estimator calibration.
47	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d7-per-cw-attribution/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
48	
49	## Wave Plan
50	
51	### Wave 1 — Foundation
52	
53	| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Notes |
54	|--------|----------------|-------|
55	| D1 task-graph-reader | Yes — new library, no shared-write conflicts | Parses tasks.md + cross-domain deps → DAG. D2, D4, D5, D6 all consume the DAG object. Lands first. |
56	| D7 per-cw-attribution | Yes — owns `cw_id` field pass-through in token-capture | Foundation because D6's estimator and the post-spawn calibration loop both require `cw_id`-tagged rows. Touches only `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` (unique owner). |
57	
58	**Gate to Wave 2**: D1 emits a well-formed DAG from a synthetic 2-task fixture; D7 `cw_id` pass-through lands in `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` and rows in `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` include the field.
59	
60	### Wave 2 — Gates (parallel-safe)
61	
62	| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Depends on |
63	|--------|----------------|-----------|
64	| D4 dep-graph validation | Yes — new library, reads DAG + task-state artifacts | D1 (DAG emitter) |
65	| D5 file-disjointness prover | Yes — new library, reads scope.md + git history | D1 (task touch-list field) |
66	| D6 pre-spawn economics estimator | Yes — new library, reads D7-tagged token-usage.jsonl | D1 (DAG), D7 (cw_id-tagged rows for calibration corpus) |
67	
68	All three are pre-spawn safety gates and purely read-only against existing artifacts. None touches command files or the orchestrator's execution path — safe to run in parallel.
69	
70	**Gate to Wave 3**: D4 + D5 gates exercised end-to-end against a synthetic 2-task fixture; D6 estimator produces a decision against a real D7-tagged dataset slice (at minimum: the existing 525-row corpus now enriched with `cw_id`).
71	
72	### Wave 3 — Integration (D2 first, then D3)
73	
74	| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Notes |
75	|--------|----------------|-------|
76	| D2 `gsd-t parallel` CLI | Runs FIRST in Wave 3 (not parallel with D3) | Wires D1 DAG + D4/D5/D6 gates + M40 orchestrator extensions into the new `parallel` subcommand. Also extends `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` with mode-aware gating math. Must be stable before D3 can call it. |
77	| D3 command-file integration | Runs AFTER D2 lands | Edits `commands/gsd-t-{execute,wave,quick,debug,integrate}.md` to dispatch via `gsd-t parallel`. D3 depends on D2's CLI surface being stable. |
78	
79	**No within-Wave-3 parallelism**: D3 starts only after D2's gate passes.
80	
81	## Shared Files & Conflict Map
82	
83	| File | Owner | Notes |
84	|------|-------|-------|
85	| `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator.js` | **D2** (parallel subcommand wiring) | D6 callsite for gating math is via the config module, not inline |
86	| `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` | **D2** (mode-aware gating math) + **D6** (estimator callsite) | D6 passes estimator result object into config; D2 owns the config file. Both touch different logical sections — D6 adds an `estimateTaskFootprint()` import, D2 adds the in-session orchestrator-CW headroom block. D6 lands in Wave 2 and leaves a clean extension point; D2 completes the wiring in Wave 3. |
87	| `commands/gsd-t-execute.md` | **D3** only | D3 owns the integration block in all 5 command files |
88	| `commands/gsd-t-wave.md` | **D3** only | |
89	| `commands/gsd-t-quick.md` | **D3** only | |
90	| `commands/gsd-t-debug.md` | **D3** only | |
91	| `commands/gsd-t-integrate.md` | **D3** only | |
92	| `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` | **D7** only | Adds optional `cw_id` field to the row writer and pass-through. No other domain touches this file in M44. |
93	| `.gsd-t/contracts/wave-join-contract.md` | **D2** owns the bump | Mode-aware gating math addition (v1.0.0 → v1.1.0) |
94	| `.gsd-t/contracts/metrics-schema-contract.md` | **D7** owns the bump | Adds optional `cw_id` field (v2 → v2.1.0) |
95	| `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md` | **D7** owns the bump | Post-spawn calibration loop wiring (v1.0.0 → v1.1.0) |
96	| `.gsd-t/contracts/headless-default-contract.md` | **D2 + D3 read-only** | Neither bumps unless adding required behavior; the v2.0.0 contract from M43 D4 covers the always-headless invariant and does not need revision for parallelism |
97	| `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-recover.cjs` | **D2 read-only** | Recovery semantics unchanged; D2 reads to understand resume surface |
98	
99	## Integration Points
100	
101	**Post-Wave-2** (before Wave 3 starts):
102	- D6 estimator runs `estimateTaskFootprint()` against a real D7-tagged dataset slice from the 525-row corpus enriched with `cw_id`. Assert: returns a decision object with fields `parallelOk`, `estimatedCwPct`, `workerCount`, `mode`. Fixture: simulate a "domain=execute, step=Wave 1" lookup.
103	- D4 + D5 gates exercised end-to-end against a synthetic 2-task fixture: two task stubs, one with declared dep on the other, one with overlapping file in touch-list. Assert D4 rejects the out-of-order request; assert D5 falls back to sequential for the overlapping pair.
104	
105	**Post-Wave-3** (before `/gsd-t-integrate`):
106	- **In-session smoke test**: invoke `gsd-t-execute` against a small multi-domain fixture using the new `parallel` path (`--mode in-session`). Assert wall-clock ≤ T/2 vs sequential baseline for a known fixture, AND zero pause/resume prompts appear in the event stream.
107	- **Unattended smoke test**: invoke `gsd-t unattended` with a multi-task milestone configuration and `--max-iterations 5`. Assert: (a) every worker completes without compaction — zero entries added to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` during the run window, (b) D7 `cw_id` tags are present in all new token-usage rows emitted during the run.
108	
109	## Skipped Partition Steps (with rationale)
110	
111	- **Step 1.5 Assumption Audit**: D6 has external-dataset reference disposition (the 525-row `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` calibration corpus + 72-event `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`). These are living files in the repo, not external service dependencies — disposition is "read as calibration input, document known-failure modes in the economics-estimator-contract." D1–D5, D7 inherit framework-internal references only. No external unlocks needed.
112	- **Step 1.6 Consumer Surface**: N/A (GSD-T is a framework package; the consumer surface is the command files and the `gsd-t parallel` CLI which are both internal).
113	- **Step 3.5 Design Brief**: N/A (no UI surface; all new surfaces are CLI and JSONL).
114	- **Step 3.6 Design Contract**: N/A (same reason as 3.5).
115	
116	## Execution Order (supervisor / solo)
117	
118	1. **Wave 1** — D1 + D7 in parallel (both Wave-1-safe, disjoint file ownership).
119	2. **Wave 1 gate** — D1 DAG emits cleanly + D7 `cw_id` field confirmed in token-capture.
120	3. **Wave 2** — D4 + D5 + D6 in parallel (all read-only against existing artifacts, no shared writes).
121	4. **Wave 2 gate** — D4/D5 synthetic fixture gates pass + D6 produces a decision from real corpus slice.
122	5. **Wave 3, step 1** — D2 alone (extends orchestrator + config; lands `gsd-t parallel` CLI; bumps wave-join-contract v1.1.0).
123	6. **Wave 3, step 2** — D3 alone (edits 5 command files to dispatch via D2).
124	7. **Wave 3 gate** — in-session + unattended smoke tests above pass.
125	8. `/gsd-t-integrate` → `/gsd-t-verify` → auto-invokes `/gsd-t-complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10.
126	9. `npm publish` → `/gsd-t-version-update-all`.
127	
128	## Known Blockers
129	
130	None currently. All M44 pre-reqs landed:
131	- Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`)
132	- Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8` / `f7de324`)
133	- Q2b compact_marker frame + visualizer badge (`8abe4ef`)
134	- Q3 turn→tool join fix (`8f4588b`)
135	- Optimization report generator (`b5edff2`)
136	- Adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`)
137	
138	Calibration corpus exists and is stable: `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (525 rows) + `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` (72 events) + `.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md` (per-CW rollup).
139	
140	Note: D7 `cw_id` enrichment of the existing corpus is a post-Wave-1 step — the 525 historical rows will not gain `cw_id` retroactively (backfill is out of scope); D6 calibration uses the subset of rows that carry `cw_id` from new spawns + a fallback to per-iter median for rows without it.
141

# M44 Partition — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)

**Status**: PARTITIONED
**Date**: 2026-04-22
**Target version**: 3.18.10
**Domains**: 7 (D1 task-graph-reader · D2 parallel-cli · D3 command-file-integration · D4 depgraph-validation · D5 file-disjointness-prover · D6 pre-spawn-economics · D7 per-cw-attribution)
**Waves**: 3 (Foundation → Gates → Integration)
**Rationale source**: M44 scope 2026-04-22 (mode-aware parallelism; see `.gsd-t/progress.md` M44 "Current Milestone" + backlog #14).

## Theme Split

- **Layer 1 — Parallel `claude -p` worker spawns**: primary lever for both modes. K workers each with a clean CW. For [unattended], this is the compaction-elimination mechanism — slicing work into smaller per-worker pieces that never approach the CW ceiling. For [in-session], it is primarily a wall-clock reducer. D2 delivers the CLI wrapping M40 orchestrator; D3 wires it into the command files.
- **Layer 2 — Parallel tasks within one worker**: weaker lever, bounded by one CW. Used when L1 isn't economic (small tasks, shared context). Same disjointness/economics gates apply at sub-iter granularity. This layer is part of D2's `--mode in-session` path.

**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt. Silent compaction is acceptable; demanding user attention is not.
- **[unattended]** Zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate. Speed is a side-benefit. Supervisor (Node, no CW) orchestrates `claude -p` workers.

## Domains

### D1 — m44-d1-task-graph-reader
**Responsibility**: parse `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md` and cross-domain dependency declarations; emit a validated DAG of independently-executable task slices. Mode-agnostic.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d1-task-graph-reader/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D2 — m44-d2-parallel-cli
**Responsibility**: new `gsd-t parallel` subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level (not just domain-level) parallelism. Honors `--mode in-session|unattended` flag (or auto-detects from caller). Both modes. Consumes D1 DAG, D4 dep-gate, D5 disjointness-gate, D6 economics gate.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d2-parallel-cli/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D3 — m44-d3-command-file-integration
**Responsibility**: `gsd-t-execute`, `gsd-t-wave`, `gsd-t-quick`, `gsd-t-debug`, `gsd-t-integrate` learn to consume the task-graph via D2 CLI and dispatch parallel workers. Both modes. Depends on D2's stable CLI surface.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d3-command-file-integration/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D4 — m44-d4-depgraph-validation
**Responsibility**: pre-spawn validator that confirms task dependencies are honored before fan-out; refuses to spawn tasks whose dependencies have not completed. Mode-agnostic. Read-only against task state artifacts.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d4-depgraph-validation/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D5 — m44-d5-file-disjointness-prover
**Responsibility**: walk each task's expected-touch list against every other concurrent task's list; prove no shared write targets before parallel spawn. Falls back to sequential when unprovable. Sources: domain `scope.md`, prior commit history for similar tasks, optional explicit `touches:` field on task stubs. Mode-agnostic.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d5-file-disjointness-prover/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D6 — m44-d6-pre-spawn-economics
**Responsibility**: query `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` for prior-similar-task token cost (matches by command + step + domain); estimate per-worker CW footprint; decide parallel-vs-sequential. Mode-aware: feeds [in-session] orchestrator-CW gate vs [unattended] per-worker-CW gate. Calibrated against the 525-row + 72-event corpus.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d6-pre-spawn-economics/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D7 — m44-d7-per-cw-attribution
**Responsibility**: ensure every spawn (parallel or sequential) tags its token-usage rows with `cw_id` so the per-CW rollup keeps working post-M44. Wire the existing `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` hook output into the supervisor's "we failed to prevent compaction" signal for [unattended] estimator calibration.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d7-per-cw-attribution/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

## Wave Plan

### Wave 1 — Foundation

| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Notes |
|--------|----------------|-------|
| D1 task-graph-reader | Yes — new library, no shared-write conflicts | Parses tasks.md + cross-domain deps → DAG. D2, D4, D5, D6 all consume the DAG object. Lands first. |
| D7 per-cw-attribution | Yes — owns `cw_id` field pass-through in token-capture | Foundation because D6's estimator and the post-spawn calibration loop both require `cw_id`-tagged rows. Touches only `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` (unique owner). |

**Gate to Wave 2**: D1 emits a well-formed DAG from a synthetic 2-task fixture; D7 `cw_id` pass-through lands in `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` and rows in `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` include the field.

### Wave 2 — Gates (parallel-safe)

| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Depends on |
|--------|----------------|-----------|
| D4 dep-graph validation | Yes — new library, reads DAG + task-state artifacts | D1 (DAG emitter) |
| D5 file-disjointness prover | Yes — new library, reads scope.md + git history | D1 (task touch-list field) |
| D6 pre-spawn economics estimator | Yes — new library, reads D7-tagged token-usage.jsonl | D1 (DAG), D7 (cw_id-tagged rows for calibration corpus) |

All three are pre-spawn safety gates and purely read-only against existing artifacts. None touches command files or the orchestrator's execution path — safe to run in parallel.

**Gate to Wave 3**: D4 + D5 gates exercised end-to-end against a synthetic 2-task fixture; D6 estimator produces a decision against a real D7-tagged dataset slice (at minimum: the existing 525-row corpus now enriched with `cw_id`).

### Wave 3 — Integration (D2 first, then D3)

| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Notes |
|--------|----------------|-------|
| D2 `gsd-t parallel` CLI | Runs FIRST in Wave 3 (not parallel with D3) | Wires D1 DAG + D4/D5/D6 gates + M40 orchestrator extensions into the new `parallel` subcommand. Also extends `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` with mode-aware gating math. Must be stable before D3 can call it. |
| D3 command-file integration | Runs AFTER D2 lands | Edits `commands/gsd-t-{execute,wave,quick,debug,integrate}.md` to dispatch via `gsd-t parallel`. D3 depends on D2's CLI surface being stable. |

**No within-Wave-3 parallelism**: D3 starts only after D2's gate passes.

## Shared Files & Conflict Map

| File | Owner | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator.js` | **D2** (parallel subcommand wiring) | D6 callsite for gating math is via the config module, not inline |
| `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` | **D2** (mode-aware gating math) + **D6** (estimator callsite) | D6 passes estimator result object into config; D2 owns the config file. Both touch different logical sections — D6 adds an `estimateTaskFootprint()` import, D2 adds the in-session orchestrator-CW headroom block. D6 lands in Wave 2 and leaves a clean extension point; D2 completes the wiring in Wave 3. |
| `commands/gsd-t-execute.md` | **D3** only | D3 owns the integration block in all 5 command files |
| `commands/gsd-t-wave.md` | **D3** only | |
| `commands/gsd-t-quick.md` | **D3** only | |
| `commands/gsd-t-debug.md` | **D3** only | |
| `commands/gsd-t-integrate.md` | **D3** only | |
| `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` | **D7** only | Adds optional `cw_id` field to the row writer and pass-through. No other domain touches this file in M44. |
| `.gsd-t/contracts/wave-join-contract.md` | **D2** owns the bump | Mode-aware gating math addition (v1.0.0 → v1.1.0) |
| `.gsd-t/contracts/metrics-schema-contract.md` | **D7** owns the bump | Adds optional `cw_id` field (v2 → v2.1.0) |
| `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md` | **D7** owns the bump | Post-spawn calibration loop wiring (v1.0.0 → v1.1.0) |
| `.gsd-t/contracts/headless-default-contract.md` | **D2 + D3 read-only** | Neither bumps unless adding required behavior; the v2.0.0 contract from M43 D4 covers the always-headless invariant and does not need revision for parallelism |
| `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-recover.cjs` | **D2 read-only** | Recovery semantics unchanged; D2 reads to understand resume surface |

## Integration Points

**Post-Wave-2** (before Wave 3 starts):
- D6 estimator runs `estimateTaskFootprint()` against a real D7-tagged dataset slice from the 525-row corpus enriched with `cw_id`. Assert: returns a decision object with fields `parallelOk`, `estimatedCwPct`, `workerCount`, `mode`. Fixture: simulate a "domain=execute, step=Wave 1" lookup.
- D4 + D5 gates exercised end-to-end against a synthetic 2-task fixture: two task stubs, one with declared dep on the other, one with overlapping file in touch-list. Assert D4 rejects the out-of-order request; assert D5 falls back to sequential for the overlapping pair.

**Post-Wave-3** (before `/gsd-t-integrate`):
- **In-session smoke test**: invoke `gsd-t-execute` against a small multi-domain fixture using the new `parallel` path (`--mode in-session`). Assert wall-clock ≤ T/2 vs sequential baseline for a known fixture, AND zero pause/resume prompts appear in the event stream.
- **Unattended smoke test**: invoke `gsd-t unattended` with a multi-task milestone configuration and `--max-iterations 5`. Assert: (a) every worker completes without compaction — zero entries added to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` during the run window, (b) D7 `cw_id` tags are present in all new token-usage rows emitted during the run.

## Skipped Partition Steps (with rationale)

- **Step 1.5 Assumption Audit**: D6 has external-dataset reference disposition (the 525-row `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` calibration corpus + 72-event `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`). These are living files in the repo, not external service dependencies — disposition is "read as calibration input, document known-failure modes in the economics-estimator-contract." D1–D5, D7 inherit framework-internal references only. No external unlocks needed.
- **Step 1.6 Consumer Surface**: N/A (GSD-T is a framework package; the consumer surface is the command files and the `gsd-t parallel` CLI which are both internal).
- **Step 3.5 Design Brief**: N/A (no UI surface; all new surfaces are CLI and JSONL).
- **Step 3.6 Design Contract**: N/A (same reason as 3.5).

## Execution Order (supervisor / solo)

1. **Wave 1** — D1 + D7 in parallel (both Wave-1-safe, disjoint file ownership).
2. **Wave 1 gate** — D1 DAG emits cleanly + D7 `cw_id` field confirmed in token-capture.
3. **Wave 2** — D4 + D5 + D6 in parallel (all read-only against existing artifacts, no shared writes).
4. **Wave 2 gate** — D4/D5 synthetic fixture gates pass + D6 produces a decision from real corpus slice.
5. **Wave 3, step 1** — D2 alone (extends orchestrator + config; lands `gsd-t parallel` CLI; bumps wave-join-contract v1.1.0).
6. **Wave 3, step 2** — D3 alone (edits 5 command files to dispatch via D2).
7. **Wave 3 gate** — in-session + unattended smoke tests above pass.
8. `/gsd-t-integrate` → `/gsd-t-verify` → auto-invokes `/gsd-t-complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10.
9. `npm publish` → `/gsd-t-version-update-all`.

## Known Blockers

None currently. All M44 pre-reqs landed:
- Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`)
- Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8` / `f7de324`)
- Q2b compact_marker frame + visualizer badge (`8abe4ef`)
- Q3 turn→tool join fix (`8f4588b`)
- Optimization report generator (`b5edff2`)
- Adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`)

Calibration corpus exists and is stable: `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (525 rows) + `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` (72 events) + `.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md` (per-CW rollup).

Note: D7 `cw_id` enrichment of the existing corpus is a post-Wave-1 step — the 525 historical rows will not gain `cw_id` retroactively (backfill is out of scope); D6 calibration uses the subset of rows that carry `cw_id` from new spawns + a fallback to per-iter median for rows without it.

# Tasks: m44-d7-per-cw-attribution

## Wave 1 — Foundation

### M44-D7-T1 — Contract skeletons: metrics-schema v2.1.0 + compaction-events v1.1.0
- **Status**: [ ] pending
- **Dependencies**: none (D7 is Wave 1, no upstream M44 deps)
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `.gsd-t/contracts/metrics-schema-contract.md` bumped to v2.1.0 with `cw_id: string (optional)` field added to the token-usage row schema section
  - `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md` bumped to v1.1.0 with `compaction_post_spawn` calibration event type added (schema: `{type, cw_id, task_id, spawn_id, estimatedCwPct, actualCwPct, ts}`)
  - Both contract versions reflect the bump date and M44-D7 as the owning domain
- **Files touched**: `.gsd-t/contracts/metrics-schema-contract.md`, `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md`

### M44-D7-T2 — `cw_id` pass-through in token-capture
- **Status**: [ ] pending
- **Dependencies**: M44-D7-T1
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` `recordSpawnRow` (and `captureSpawn`) accept an optional `cw_id` field in the row options object
  - When `cw_id` is provided, it is written to the JSONL row; when absent, field is omitted (not null)
  - All existing callers (no `cw_id` arg) continue to work and produce identical output to pre-D7 (backward-compatible)
  - Existing test suite passes without modification
- **Files touched**: `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`

### M44-D7-T3 — Calibration hook: compact-detector → supervisor signal
- **Status**: [ ] pending
- **Dependencies**: M44-D7-T2
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` exists as a SessionStart hook handler
  - On `source=compact`, the hook: reads active spawn context (if any) from `.gsd-t/.unattended/state.json`; derives `actualCwPct` from the compaction event's `input_tokens` vs CW ceiling; appends a `compaction_post_spawn` calibration event to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`
  - When no active unattended spawn is found, hook exits with code 0 and writes nothing (silent no-op)
  - Hook is safe to register alongside `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` (both fire on SessionStart; independent; no conflicts)
- **Files touched**: `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` (new)

### M44-D7-T4 — Unit test suite
- **Status**: [ ] pending
- **Dependencies**: M44-D7-T3
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` covers: `recordSpawnRow` with `cw_id` present (field in row), `recordSpawnRow` without `cw_id` (field absent, other fields unchanged), calibration hook with active spawn (calibration event written), calibration hook without active spawn (no-op, no file written)
  - All tests pass via `npm test`
- **Files touched**: `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` (new)

### M44-D7-T5 — Doc-ripple + tests-pass commit
- **Status**: [ ] pending
- **Dependencies**: M44-D7-T4
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `docs/requirements.md` updated with §"M44 Per-CW Attribution" requirement entry
  - `docs/architecture.md` updated to reflect `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` and the `cw_id` field in token-capture
  - All existing tests still pass; Wave 1 D7 gate met (`cw_id` field confirmed in new token-capture rows)
  - `.gsd-t/progress.md` decision log entry added for D7 Wave 1 foundation complete
- **Files touched**: `docs/requirements.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `.gsd-t/progress.md`

content.content:
502:## M44 Task-Graph Reader (D1 — execute phase 2026-04-22)
504:Milestone 44 D1 ships the shared DAG that all downstream M44 domains (D2 parallel CLI, D3 command-file integration, D4 dep-graph validation, D5 file-disjointness prover, D6 pre-spawn economics) consume. Mode-agnostic: produces only a graph, owns no in-session vs unattended branching.
508:| REQ-M44-D1-01 | `bin/gsd-t-task-graph.cjs` parses every `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md` into a typed in-memory DAG: nodes `{id, domain, wave, title, status, deps, touches}`, edges `{from, to}`, `ready` mask, `byId` index, `warnings[]` | m44-d1-task-graph-reader | **complete (2026-04-22)** — 22/22 unit tests pass |
509:| REQ-M44-D1-02 | Cycle detection mandatory: throws `TaskGraphCycleError` with `.cycle: string[]` path on any circular dep — 3-task ring AND self-loop covered | m44-d1-task-graph-reader | **complete (2026-04-22)** — iterative three-color DFS; tests 3 + 4 |
510:| REQ-M44-D1-03 | Touch-list resolution: prefer task `**Touches**` / `**Files touched**`, fall back to domain `scope.md` `## Files Owned`, fall back to `[]` + warning | m44-d1-task-graph-reader | **complete (2026-04-22)** — both fallback paths tested |
511:| REQ-M44-D1-04 | Status markers: `[ ]` pending / `[x]` done / `[-]` skipped / `[!]` failed; unknown markers → pending + warning. Only `done` deps satisfy dependents (skipped/failed do NOT) | m44-d1-task-graph-reader | **complete (2026-04-22)** — covered by ready-mask tests |
512:| REQ-M44-D1-05 | Read-only invariant: never writes to `tasks.md`, `scope.md`, or any contract file during build/query | m44-d1-task-graph-reader | **complete (2026-04-22)** — sync `readFileSync` only, no `writeFileSync` calls |
513:| REQ-M44-D1-06 | Performance: parse + cycle-check + ready-mask in < 200 ms for 100-domain / 1000-task project | m44-d1-task-graph-reader | **complete (2026-04-22)** — measured 6 ms on 50-domain/250-task synthetic; 3 ms on this repo |
514:| REQ-M44-D1-07 | CLI debugging surface: `gsd-t graph --output json` (pretty JSON) and `--output table` (id/domain/wave/status/ready/deps), backward-compat with existing `graph index/status/query` | m44-d1-task-graph-reader | **complete (2026-04-22)** — verified live: 33 tasks · 36 edges · 2 ready |
517:- `task-graph-contract.md` v1.0.0 — locks DAG schema; downstream M44 domains may begin implementation against this contract.
519:## M44 Per-CW Attribution (D7 — Wave 1 foundation, 2026-04-22)
521:Milestone 44 D7 ships the per-Context-Window attribution surface that downstream consumers (D6 estimator calibration, the per-CW rollup in `gsd-t metrics`, the optimization report) need to keep working post-M44. Without `cw_id` on token-usage rows, every iter looks like a single CW even when Claude Code compacted mid-run; the calibration loop also needs a post-spawn signal so D6 can self-correct from the delta between predicted and observed CW utilization.
525:| REQ-M44-D7-01 | `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` accepts an optional `cw_id` field on `recordSpawnRow` / `captureSpawn`. When supplied, written to the JSONL row; when absent, omitted (NOT null, NOT ""). Pass-through only — wrapper does not derive `cw_id`. | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** — 15/15 existing m41-token-capture tests still pass unchanged |
526:| REQ-M44-D7-02 | `metrics-schema-contract.md` bumped v2 → v2.1.0 documenting `cw_id` field + derivation rules (unattended: `cw_id == spawn_id`; in-session: `session_id + ":" + compaction_index`) | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** |
527:| REQ-M44-D7-03 | `compaction-events-contract.md` bumped v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 adding `compaction_post_spawn` calibration event type appended to the same sink (`compactions.jsonl`); pairs `estimatedCwPct` with `actualCwPct` | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** |
528:| REQ-M44-D7-04 | `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` SessionStart hook: on `source=compact`, correlates with active spawn from `.gsd-t/.unattended/state.json`, derives `actualCwPct` from `payload.input_tokens` ÷ CW ceiling, appends one calibration row. Silent no-op when no active spawn (supervisor not running). | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** — 19/19 unit tests in `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` |
529:| REQ-M44-D7-05 | Calibration hook safe to register alongside `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` — both fire on SessionStart, both write to the same sink, neither reads/writes the other's rows. Hook always exits 0 (throwing breaks Claude Code session start). | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** — coexistence test verifies v1.0.0 detector rows remain intact when calibration row appended |
530:| REQ-M44-D7-06 | Backward compatibility: every pre-D7 v2 row remains a valid v2.1.0 row. Pre-D7 callers (no `cw_id` supplied) produce byte-identical output. Historical `token-usage.jsonl` rows are NOT backfilled with `cw_id`. | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** |
533:- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v2.1.0 — adds optional `cw_id` field; documents producer ownership (M44 D7) and derivation rules

content.content:
591:## Task-Graph Reader (M44 D1, v3.18.10+)
593:`bin/gsd-t-task-graph.cjs` — a zero-external-dep CommonJS module that parses every `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md` (and falls back to each domain's `scope.md` `## Files Owned` for missing touch lists) into a typed in-memory DAG. This is the **single shared input** for every M44 parallelism domain — D2 `gsd-t parallel`, D3 command-file integration, D4 dep-graph validation, D5 file-disjointness prover, D6 pre-spawn economics — none of which re-parse `tasks.md` themselves.
628:## Per-CW Attribution (M44 D7, v3.18.10+)
630:Per-Context-Window (CW) attribution lets the optimization report, the per-CW rollup in `gsd-t metrics`, and D6's pre-spawn estimator distinguish multiple CWs within one iter — necessary because Claude Code can compact mid-run, silently splitting one iter into two CWs that pre-D7 metrics treated as a single unit.
634:   │ supplies cw_id (= spawn_id for unattended; = session_id+":"+compaction_index for in-session)
637:   │ pass-through; serializes cw_id into the JSONL row when supplied
639:.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl   (schema v2.1.0 — cw_id optional)
645:   └──> scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js   (v1.1.0 — calibration row)
650:        ({type: "compaction_post_spawn", cw_id, task_id, spawn_id,
654:[Omitted long matching line]
656:**`scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js`** — zero-external-dep SessionStart hook handler that complements (does not replace) `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js`. Both hooks fire on the same payload; both write to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`; neither reads or writes the other's rows. The calibration hook silently no-ops when:
665:**Contracts**: `.gsd-t/contracts/metrics-schema-contract.md` v2.1.0 (`cw_id` field), `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md` v1.1.0 (calibration event)
667:**Tests**: `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` (19 tests covering pass-through with/without `cw_id`, calibration hook with/without active spawn, malformed state, non-compact sources, derivation fallback to `compactMetadata.preTokens`, ceiling overrides, coexistence with v1.0.0 detector rows, and zero-deps verification).

Exports `run`, `scanSessionFile`, `boundaryToRow`, `deriveSessionsRoot`, `loadExistingKeys`, `rowKey` for testability. Row preserves optional fields from `compactMetadata`: `trigger` (auto|manual), `preTokens`, `postTokens`, `durationMs` — but older Claude Code archives do not consistently emit `postTokens`, so missing optional fields are simply omitted rather than guessed (documented in contract as a consumer invariant). Backfill rows use `logicalParentUuid` as the `prior_session_id` — it's the last pre-compact message UUID, the closest stable boundary anchor the archive exposes for in-place compactions (documented explicitly). 4 new tests (13/13 pass total across M44 test file): scanner dry-run no-writes, scanner --write + dedup on re-run, missing sessions root exits cleanly, slug derivation matches Claude Code convention. **Live backfill run**: 112 GSD-T session files scanned, **72 historical compaction boundaries** extracted spanning 2026-04-04 through 2026-04-21, written to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`. This is the first persistent record of how many times Claude Code auto-compacted mid-run in this repo — 72 events over 18 days confirms the M43 debrief observation that CW transitions were the dominant invisible boundary in the token-accounting pipeline. Contract `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md` updated with the 72-event empirical note + the `prior_session_id`-on-backfill semantic.
143	
144	- 2026-04-22 20:10: [quick] compaction detector hook + contract + tests (M44 pre-req). New `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` is a `SessionStart` hook that reads the stdin JSON payload, and — only when `source === "compact"` — appends one NDJSON row to `<cwd>/.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`. startup / resume are no-ops. Fail-open contract: exits 0 on every code path (throwing here would break Claude Code session startup). 1 MiB stdin cap + path-traversal guard (resolved output path must stay under `<cwd>/.gsd-t/metrics/`) + off-switch (no `<cwd>/.gsd-t/` → silent no-op; the hook never creates that directory — opt-in). Row schema v1: `{ts, schemaVersion, session_id, prior_session_id, source, cwd, hook}`; `source` is `"compact"` for live captures and reserved `"compact-backfill"` for the historical scanner (landing in a follow-up commit). Wired into `~/.claude/settings.json` as a fourth `SessionStart` matcher alongside the existing `gsd-t-update-check.js` and `gsd-t-heartbeat.js` hooks (neither touched); reads the installed package via `$(npm root -g)/@tekyzinc/gsd-t/scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` with a presence guard so sessions in non-GSD-T projects short-circuit silently. New contract `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md` v1.0.0: rationale (CW boundaries invisible today → Run→Iter→CW→Turn→Tool hierarchy silently collapses), row schema, hook lifecycle, guardrails, backfill semantics, wiring pointer. New `test/m44-compaction-detector.test.js` — 9 tests all pass: source=compact writes row; source=startup no-op; source=resume no-op; malformed stdin no-throw; 1 MiB cap triggers abort; path-traversal cwd rejected (relative paths + fallback), off-switch holds when no `.gsd-t/`; empty stdin still exits 0; three appends → three rows (append-only, no overwrite). Named `m44-*` because M44 (Generalized Cross-Domain Parallel Task Orchestrator) consumes these rows for CW-scoped attribution. Scanner (historical backfill from `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/*.jsonl`) lands in a sibling commit.

in-session: `session_id + ":" + compaction_index` per the calibration hook's compact-fire counter), `compaction-events-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 (adds `compaction_post_spawn` event type, calibration hook lifecycle, guardrails, three-hook wiring matrix). **Test suite**: `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` — 19 tests, all green isolated: cw_id present → field serialized, cw_id numeric → coerced to string, cw_id absent/null/empty → field OMITTED (not null, not ""), captureSpawn forwards cw_id, `_buildJsonlRecord` cw_id branch matches session_id pattern, calibration hook with active spawn → calibration row written with correct task_id + estimatedCwPct + actualCwPct ~0.97 for 194000/200000, fallback to compactMetadata.preTokens, cwCeilingTokens override (95000/100000 = 0.95), no state.json → no row written, status !== running → no-op, malformed json → silent, source !== compact → no-op, missing input_tokens → no-op, empty/non-json stdin → no-op, non-absolute cwd → no-op, calibration row appends alongside v1.0.0 detector rows in same sink, zero ext deps. **Hard constraints all met** (per `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d7-per-cw-attribution/constraints.md`): backward-compatible cw_id (existing callers unchanged, absent ≠ null), silent no-op when no active spawn, zero ext deps, calibration event includes BOTH estimatedCwPct AND actualCwPct, contract bumps in D7's commit, `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` NOT modified. **Out of scope** (deferred): backfilling pre-D7 token-usage.jsonl rows with cw_id (525 historical rows; D6 falls back to per-iter median); economics logic (D6); parallel dispatch (D2); CW boundary detection algorithm beyond the spawn_id == cw_id rule. **D1 ran in parallel** (also Wave 1 Foundation per partition map: D1 + D7 both no-dep). The continue-here file from earlier in the session claimed M44 was already in the backlog — it was not; grep came back empty. Now landed. Objectives per mode: [in-session] speed + minimize-compaction (hard rule: NEVER throw interactive pause/resume prompts); [unattended] zero compaction across autonomous M1→M10 run. Three pre-existing invariants (file-disjointness, 100% auto-merges, pre-spawn economics) apply to both modes identically — they're correctness, not mode. Mode-specific gating math: [in-session] orchestrator-CW headroom (worker summaries land in user-facing CW; bound fan-out width AND result-envelope size) vs [unattended] per-worker-CW headroom (supervisor is Node process with no CW; bind per-`claude -p` worker CW ceiling). Two delivery layers (L1: parallel `claude -p` worker spawns, L2: parallel tasks within one worker) are mode-agnostic. **Token-cost honesty in the entry**: parallel is approximately token-neutral in steady state (total input/output same, wall-clock is the primary win; `max` instead of `sum`), and token-positive only in the case where sequential would have compacted (compaction-induced re-input drops to zero). Rate-limit pressure is explicitly NOT a con — hitting the 5-hour ceiling because 20× more work got done is the goal. 7 proposed domains (D1 task-graph reader, D2 `gsd-t parallel` CLI, D3 command-file integration, D4 dep-graph validation, D5 file-disjointness prover, D6 pre-spawn economics + cost estimator consuming the 525-row calibration corpus, D7 per-CW attribution + compaction-event integration). User framing that drove this: "In session, there's two clear objectives, speed and reduce compaction as much as is possible" + "Unattended must run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and zero compaction. This is critical, but not for all builds. Many I will want to run in session because I don't have all the requirements defined or I want to be able to interrupt." Success criteria: [in-session] T/2 wall-clock reduction with N workers and zero pause prompts; [unattended] zero compaction events logged during a measured autonomous chain (baseline: 72 compactions over 18 days of mixed-mode work from Q2a backfill).

- 2026-04-22 21:50: [quick] token-usage optimization report generator — `gsd-t report tokens [--date YYYY-MM-DD] [--out PATH]` writes a markdown report at `.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-{DATE}.md` organized per the canonical 5-level hierarchy `Run → Iter → CW → Turn → Tool`, with CW (context window) as the primary optimization unit (peer of Iter, not a partition of it). New `bin/gsd-t-report-tokens.cjs` (430 lines) exports `generateReport`, `groupIntoCWs`, `rollupCW`, `topNExpensiveTurns`, `groupCompactionEvents`, `renderMarkdown`. CW grouping rule: each distinct `session_id` in `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` = one CW; compactions in `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` whose `prior_session_id` matches a CW mark `endedBy: compaction (auto|manual)` (manual when `trigger=manual`, auto otherwise; missing trigger defaults to auto with a footnote dagger). CWs without a following compaction get `iter end` (if a later CW exists) or `run end` (if it's the last in the data). Report sections: **A** per-CW rollup (PRIMARY) — `CW# | Iter | Start | Turns | In | Out | CacheR | CacheC | Avg Out/turn | Peak Ctx% | Ended-by` with totals + average-turns-per-CW + compaction rate; **B** tool-tokens rollup via `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs::joinTurnsAndEvents` + `aggregateByTool` — B.1 by-tool table + B.2 tool×command cross-tab (top 10 tools × top 5 commands by total tokens); **C** top-20 expensive turns sorted by `input + output` descending (input-heavy = context bloat, output-heavy = generation cost — both columns visible); **D** all compaction events with `ts | source | iter | trigger | preTokens | postTokens | durationMs | active command | active domain/task` enriched with the last turn's command/domain/task before the compaction `ts`. **No cost columns anywhere** (Max subscription = $0); **idempotent** (same date overwrites same path; output deterministic modulo the `Generated:` ISO line); **never crashes on missing data** (missing `compactions.jsonl` → "no compaction events recorded"; failed tool attribution → "Tool attribution unavailable"). CLI dispatch: new `case "report"` block in `bin/gsd-t.js` introduces nested-subcommand pattern (`gsd-t report tokens …`); help text bumped. Tests: new `test/m44-report-tokens.test.js` — **13/13 pass**: groupIntoCWs (0/2 compactions, live+backfill dedup, missing-trigger flag), rollupCW (sums + avg + peak + null ctxPct), topNExpensiveTurns (≥N + <N), groupCompactionEvents activity enrichment, generateReport end-to-end (4 sections + correct CW count + run-end + missing-compactions survival + idempotence). Full suite **1748/1749 pass** — 1 fail is pre-existing unrelated (`m43-milestone-complete-detection` asserts on live `progress.md` content — stale fixture). **Live report on this repo** (`.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md`): 1 CW with all 523 turn rows of the current session_id, top tool `no-tool` (285 of 286 attributed turns landed there because most turns have zero matched tool events) followed by Bash/Edit/Read/ScheduleWakeup. Section D shows the full 72-event compaction history (61 auto + 11 manual, all backfill) — the historical record M44's pre-req scanner produced. **Surprise**: only 1 CW in the per-CW rollup for the entire dataset because every token-usage row shares one session_id (`a5ee3b8e-1a77`); the 72 compactions are all _ancestor_ events (their `prior_session_id` doesn't match any in our turn set), so they show only in Section D and not as CW boundaries. This is correct given the data shape (all 523 rows landed in one Stop-hook capture window). Red Team skipped per quick spec — V1 analyst tool, not user-facing critical path. Files: `bin/gsd-t-report-tokens.cjs` (new, 430 lines), `bin/gsd-t.js` (+59 lines: report dispatch + help line), `test/m44-report-tokens.test.js` (new, 280 lines), `.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md` (live output, 156 lines).

- 2026-04-22 21:30: [quick] orchestrator default `--max-parallel` is now adaptive — `floor(freemem / 2GB)` clamped to floor 3, ceiling 15. Reverts the static `10` from `67c520a` which was calibrated for the maintainer's high-RAM Mac and would risk OOM on smaller machines (10 × ~1.5–2 GB resident per `claude -p` worker). Static `DEFAULTS.maxParallel` drops back to `3` (the safe floor). New `computeAdaptiveMaxParallel(freeBytes)` in `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` is invoked when no project `orchestrator.config.json` sets `maxParallel`, no `--max-parallel` flag, and no `GSD_T_MAX_PARALLEL` env. Precedence chain: `default(3) < adaptive(floor(free/2GB), [3..15]) < config-file < CLI < env`. New `loadConfig` return field `maxParallelSource` exposes which level decided ('adaptive' | 'config-file' | 'cli' | 'env') for observability. Help text updated; wave-join contract updated to "default: adaptive — floor(freemem / 2GB) clamped to floor 3 / ceiling 15". Tests: 4 new in `test/m40-orchestrator-config.test.js` covering adaptive math at 0/1/4/8/16/20/64 GB free + NaN guard, plus a regression test asserting `DEFAULTS.maxParallel === 3` (the safe floor). All 15/15 config tests + 58/58 across the full M40 orchestrator suite pass. Existing `.gsd-t/orchestrator.config.json` overrides still take precedence — power users (incl. this maintainer) can pin `maxParallel: 10` per-project without touching defaults.

- 2026-04-22 20:25: [quick] compaction historical scanner from ~/.claude/projects/ (M44 pre-req, part 2 of 2). New `scripts/gsd-t-compaction-scanner.js` scans every `.jsonl` under `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/` (slug derived from `projectDir` — `/` → `-`, matching Claude Code's encoding), filters rows where `type === "system" && subtype === "compact_boundary"`, converts them to canonical compaction rows with `source: "compact-backfill"`, dedups against existing `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` on `(ts, session_id)`, sorts by ts ascending, and — only when `--write` is passed — appends. **Default is DRY-RUN**; the `--write` flag is mandatory for mutation. Optional flags: `--project-dir DIR`, `--sessions-root DIR` (override), `--limit N` (diagnostic cap), `-h/--help`. Exports `run`, `scanSessionFile`, `boundaryToRow`, `deriveSessionsRoot`, `loadExistingKeys`, `rowKey` for testability. Row preserves optional fields from `compactMetadata`: `trigger` (auto|manual), `preTokens`, `postTokens`, `durationMs` — but older Claude Code archives do not consistently emit `postTokens`, so missing optional fields are simply omitted rather than guessed (documented in contract as a consumer invariant). Backfill rows use `logicalParentUuid` as the `prior_session_id` — it's the last pre-compact message UUID, the closest stable boundary anchor the archive exposes for in-place compactions (documented explicitly). 4 new tests (13/13 pass total across M44 test file): scanner dry-run no-writes, scanner --write + dedup on re-run, missing sessions root exits cleanly, slug derivation matches Claude Code convention. **Live backfill run**: 112 GSD-T session files scanned, **72 historical compaction boundaries** extracted spanning 2026-04-04 through 2026-04-21, written to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`. This is the first persistent record of how many times Claude Code auto-compacted mid-run in this repo — 72 events over 18 days confirms the M43 debrief observation that CW transitions were the dominant invisible boundary in the token-accounting pipeline. Contract `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md` updated with the 72-event empirical note + the `prior_session_id`-on-backfill semantic.

- 2026-04-22 20:10: [quick] compaction detector hook + contract + tests (M44 pre-req). New `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` is a `SessionStart` hook that reads the stdin JSON payload, and — only when `source === "compact"` — appends one NDJSON row to `<cwd>/.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`. startup / resume are no-ops. Fail-open contract: exits 0 on every code path (throwing here would break Claude Code session startup). 1 MiB stdin cap + path-traversal guard (resolved output path must stay under `<cwd>/.gsd-t/metrics/`) + off-switch (no `<cwd>/.gsd-t/` → silent no-op; the hook never creates that directory — opt-in). Row schema v1: `{ts, schemaVersion, session_id, prior_session_id, source, cwd, hook}`; `source` is `"compact"` for live captures and reserved `"compact-backfill"` for the historical scanner (landing in a follow-up commit). Wired into `~/.claude/settings.json` as a fourth `SessionStart` matcher alongside the existing `gsd-t-update-check.js` and `gsd-t-heartbeat.js` hooks (neither touched); reads the installed package via `$(npm root -g)/@tekyzinc/gsd-t/scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` with a presence guard so sessions in non-GSD-T projects short-circuit silently. New contract `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md` v1.0.0: rationale (CW boundaries invisible today → Run→Iter→CW→Turn→Tool hierarchy silently collapses), row schema, hook lifecycle, guardrails, backfill semantics, wiring pointer. New `test/m44-compaction-detector.test.js` — 9 tests all pass: source=compact writes row; source=startup no-op; source=resume no-op; malformed stdin no-throw; 1 MiB cap triggers abort; path-traversal cwd rejected (relative paths + fallback), off-switch holds when no `.gsd-t/`; empty stdin still exits 0; three appends → three rows (append-only, no overwrite). Named `m44-*` because M44 (Generalized Cross-Domain Parallel Task Orchestrator) consumes these rows for CW-scoped attribution. Scanner (historical backfill from `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/*.jsonl`) lands in a sibling commit.

--- a/docs/requirements.md
+++ b/docs/requirements.md
@@ -515,3 +515,25 @@ Milestone 44 D1 ships the shared DAG that all downstream M44 domains (D2 paralle
 
 Supporting contract:
 - `task-graph-contract.md` v1.0.0 — locks DAG schema; downstream M44 domains may begin implementation against this contract.
+
+## M44 Per-CW Attribution (D7 — Wave 1 foundation, 2026-04-22)
+
+Milestone 44 D7 ships the per-Context-Window attribution surface that downstream consumers (D6 estimator calibration, the per-CW rollup in `gsd-t metrics`, the optimization report) need to keep working post-M44. Without `cw_id` on token-usage rows, every iter looks like a single CW even when Claude Code compacted mid-run; the calibration loop also needs a post-spawn signal so D6 can self-correct from the delta between predicted and observed CW utilization.
+
+| REQ-ID | Requirement Summary | Domain | Status |
+|--------|---------------------|--------|--------|
+| REQ-M44-D7-01 | `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` accepts an optional `cw_id` field on `recordSpawnRow` / `captureSpawn`. When supplied, written to the JSONL row; when absent, omitted (NOT null, NOT ""). Pass-through only — wrapper does not derive `cw_id`. | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** — 15/15 existing m41-token-capture tests still pass unchanged |
+| REQ-M44-D7-02 | `metrics-schema-contract.md` bumped v2 → v2.1.0 documenting `cw_id` field + derivation rules (unattended: `cw_id == spawn_id`; in-session: `session_id + ":" + compaction_index`) | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** |
+| REQ-M44-D7-03 | `compaction-events-contract.md` bumped v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 adding `compaction_post_spawn` calibration event type appended to the same sink (`compactions.jsonl`); pairs `estimatedCwPct` with `actualCwPct` | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** |
+| REQ-M44-D7-04 | `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` SessionStart hook: on `source=compact`, correlates with active spawn from `.gsd-t/.unattended/state.json`, derives `actualCwPct` from `payload.input_tokens` ÷ CW ceiling, appends one calibration row. Silent no-op when no active spawn (supervisor not running). | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** — 19/19 unit tests in `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` |
+| REQ-M44-D7-05 | Calibration hook safe to register alongside `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` — both fire on SessionStart, both write to the same sink, neither reads/writes the other's rows. Hook always exits 0 (throwing breaks Claude Code session start). | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** — coexistence test verifies v1.0.0 detector rows remain intact when calibration row appended |
+| REQ-M44-D7-06 | Backward compatibility: every pre-D7 v2 row remains a valid v2.1.0 row. Pre-D7 callers (no `cw_id` supplied) produce byte-identical output. Historical `token-usage.jsonl` rows are NOT backfilled with `cw_id`. | m44-d7-per-cw-attribution | **complete (2026-04-22)** |
+
+Supporting contracts:
+- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v2.1.0 — adds optional `cw_id` field; documents producer ownership (M44 D7) and derivation rules
+- `compaction-events-contract.md` v1.1.0 — adds `compaction_post_spawn` calibration event; documents the calibration hook's lifecycle and guardrails
+
+Out of scope for D7:
+- Backfilling historical `token-usage.jsonl` rows with `cw_id` (consumers fall back to per-iter median for pre-D7 rows)
+- Modifying `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` (D7 adds a companion hook only)
+- Any economics logic (D6) or parallel dispatch logic (D2)

- **Dependencies**: M44-D1-T1

### M44-D1-T3 — Unit test suite
- **Status**: [ ] pending
