# Tasks: m44-d7-per-cw-attribution

## Wave 1 — Foundation

### M44-D7-T1 — Contract skeletons: metrics-schema v2.1.0 + compaction-events v1.1.0
- **Status**: [ ] pending
- **Dependencies**: none (D7 is Wave 1, no upstream M44 deps)
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `.gsd-t/contracts/metrics-schema-contract.md` bumped to v2.1.0 with `cw_id: string (optional)` field added to the token-usage row schema section
  - `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md` bumped to v1.1.0 with `compaction_post_spawn` calibration event type added (schema: `{type, cw_id, task_id, spawn_id, estimatedCwPct, actualCwPct, ts}`)
  - Both contract versions reflect the bump date and M44-D7 as the owning domain
- **Files touched**: `.gsd-t/contracts/metrics-schema-contract.md`, `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md`

### M44-D7-T2 — `cw_id` pass-through in token-capture
- **Status**: [ ] pending
- **Dependencies**: M44-D7-T1
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` `recordSpawnRow` (and `captureSpawn`) accept an optional `cw_id` field in the row options object
  - When `cw_id` is provided, it is written to the JSONL row; when absent, field is omitted (not null)
  - All existing callers (no `cw_id` arg) continue to work and produce identical output to pre-D7 (backward-compatible)
  - Existing test suite passes without modification
- **Files touched**: `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`

### M44-D7-T3 — Calibration hook: compact-detector → supervisor signal
- **Status**: [ ] pending
- **Dependencies**: M44-D7-T2
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` exists as a SessionStart hook handler
  - On `source=compact`, the hook: reads active spawn context (if any) from `.gsd-t/.unattended/state.json`; derives `actualCwPct` from the compaction event's `input_tokens` vs CW ceiling; appends a `compaction_post_spawn` calibration event to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`
  - When no active unattended spawn is found, hook exits with code 0 and writes nothing (silent no-op)
  - Hook is safe to register alongside `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` (both fire on SessionStart; independent; no conflicts)
- **Files touched**: `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` (new)

### M44-D7-T4 — Unit test suite
- **Status**: [ ] pending
- **Dependencies**: M44-D7-T3
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` covers: `recordSpawnRow` with `cw_id` present (field in row), `recordSpawnRow` without `cw_id` (field absent, other fields unchanged), calibration hook with active spawn (calibration event written), calibration hook without active spawn (no-op, no file written)
  - All tests pass via `npm test`
- **Files touched**: `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` (new)

### M44-D7-T5 — Doc-ripple + tests-pass commit
- **Status**: [ ] pending
- **Dependencies**: M44-D7-T4
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `docs/requirements.md` updated with §"M44 Per-CW Attribution" requirement entry
  - `docs/architecture.md` updated to reflect `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` and the `cw_id` field in token-capture
  - All existing tests still pass; Wave 1 D7 gate met (`cw_id` field confirmed in new token-capture rows)
  - `.gsd-t/progress.md` decision log entry added for D7 Wave 1 foundation complete
- **Files touched**: `docs/requirements.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `.gsd-t/progress.md`

All 19 assertions inside D7 test pass (lines 1564–1582), then the file gets marked `✖` at 54.8s because there's a handle keeping the test file alive past the 54-second mark. The failure is exit-signal from the runner, not a test assertion failure.

Let me check the test file for handles:

input.description: Find child process spawn in test

content.text:
I see — `require()` runs the top-level code including `process.stdin.setEncoding("utf8")` and attaches stdin listeners. When loaded via `require` in the test, it attaches listeners to the test process's stdin. When running inside the Node test runner with `--test-isolation=process`, each test file gets its own child process — but its stdin stays open awaiting the parent runner's ping. This keeps the event loop alive.

The test passes (19/19 pass), but the process doesn't exit, so the Node test runner kills it after a timeout (55s) and marks the file `✖`.

This is a pre-existing behavior of the hook itself — but other hook-handler modules must have the same issue. Let me check compact-detector:

Both hooks have the same pattern. Let me check if compact-detector test has the same issue — if it does, this is a pre-existing test infrastructure issue. In the full log, M44 compaction detector hook tests completed in 12.4 sec:

content.text:
Compaction detector tests completed successfully (12.4s — slower than D7 but nowhere near 55s). The difference: compact-detector test suite uses child processes (`spawnSync`) to invoke the hook, but D7 uses `require('../scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js')` which imports the module and triggers the top-level stdin listener registration in the test's own process. That registration keeps the event loop alive.

Quick fix: guard the top-level stdin handlers so they only fire when the module is run directly, not when required. Let me check:

Wrap the top-level stdin registration in `require.main === module` so it only runs when the script is invoked directly as a hook, not when imported by tests. This is the standard Node.js pattern and matches the other hook better (compact-detector's tests avoid it by spawning subprocesses).

content.content:
118	## Decision Log
119	
120	> Prior decision log entries preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/*/progress.md` — see archive snapshots (most recently `M40-external-task-orchestrator-2026-04-20/progress.md`) for pre-M40 history.
121	
122	- 2026-04-22 23:00: [execute M44-D7 · unattended Wave 1] M44 D7 per-CW attribution complete — 5/5 tasks shipped in sequence (T1 metrics-schema v2.1.0 + compaction-events v1.1.0 contract bumps `7c3e571` → T2 cw_id pass-through in `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` `3d784ed` → T3 calibration hook `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` `64301ed` → T4 unit test suite `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` 19/19 isolated → T5 doc-ripple this commit). **Token-capture wrapper** — `recordSpawnRow` and `captureSpawn` now accept optional `cw_id` opt; `_buildJsonlRecord` serializes it only when supplied (`if (cw_id != null && cw_id !== '') rec.cw_id = String(cw_id);` — same pattern as the existing `session_id` / `turn_id` branches, so pre-D7 callers produce byte-identical rows). 15/15 existing `m41-token-capture.test.js` tests still pass with no modifications. **Calibration hook** — new SessionStart handler that runs alongside (not replacing) `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js`. On `payload.source === "compact"` it reads `.gsd-t/.unattended/state.json`, derives `spawn_id` (sessionId fallback chain), `cw_id` (= spawn_id for unattended workers), `task_id` (activeTask / task_id / currentTask fallback chain), `estimatedCwPct` (D6's pre-spawn prediction if recorded), `cwCeilingTokens` (defaults to 200000), then derives `actualCwPct` from `payload.input_tokens` (or nested `compactMetadata.preTokens`) divided by the ceiling, clamped to [0, 2.0]. Appends one `{type: "compaction_post_spawn", schemaVersion: 1, ts, cw_id, task_id, spawn_id, estimatedCwPct, actualCwPct}` row to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`. **Silent no-op** when payload isn't a compact source, when `.gsd-t/` is missing, when `state.json` is missing/unparseable/`status !== "running"`, when no `spawn_id` derivable, when no `input_tokens` derivable, or when `cwd` is non-absolute. Always exits 0 (throwing breaks Claude Code SessionStart). Zero ext deps, 1 MiB stdin cap, path-traversal guard mirroring the detector. **Coexistence verified by test**: pre-existing v1.0.0 detector rows (`source: "compact"`, no `type` field) remain intact when calibration row appended; input.new_string:
> Prior decision log entries preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/*/progress.md` — see archive snapshots (most recently `M40-external-task-orchestrator-2026-04-20/progress.md`) for pre-M40 history.

- 2026-04-23 00:10: [execute M44 · unattended Wave 1 worker iter 2] Wave 1 closeout — D7 T4 test file (`test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js`, 19 tests) committed; D7 T5 doc-ripple already landed in-tree (requirements + architecture + progress log) now committed alongside; D1 T1-T5 and D7 T1-T5 task status flipped from `[ ] pending` to `[x] done (· commit `XXX`)` in both `tasks.md` files so the task-graph reader now reports them as satisfied deps for Wave 2. **Fix landed in same commit**: `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` top-level stdin wiring wrapped in `require.main === module` guard. Root cause: when the hook is `require()`d by a test file (as `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` does to exercise the `handle()` export directly), the module-level `process.stdin.setEncoding('utf8')` + `stdin.on('data'/'end')` listeners attach to the *test process's* stdin and keep the Node event loop alive indefinitely. All 19 D7 assertions pass in ~400ms isolated, but in the `npm test` full-suite run the file hangs on the unclosed stdin handle until the test runner kills it at its internal timeout (54885ms observed), which flagged the file as `✖` even though every sub-test was green. The `require.main === module` guard is the standard Node pattern for "only run top-level code when invoked as a script" and is functionally invisible in production (Claude Code invokes hooks via `node scripts/…` = `require.main === module` is true). After fix: 69/69 pass across D1 + D7 + m41-token-capture + compact-detector in 566ms combined. **Wave 1 gate status**: D1 DAG + D7 `cw_id` field + calibration hook all land; `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` new rows carry `cw_id` when supplied; `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` can now receive `compaction_post_spawn` calibration rows. Wave 2 (D4 + D5 + D6, all parallel-safe) unblocked.

- 2026-04-22 23:00: [execute M44-D7 · unattended Wave 1]

# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: M44 IN PROGRESS — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)
## Date: 2026-04-22
## Version: 3.17.10

## Current Milestone

**M44 Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)** — IN PROGRESS (7 proposed domains, scoped in backlog #14). Target: v3.18.10.

**Goal**: Deliver task-level parallelism to **both** execution modes on equal footing, with mode-aware gating math. Unattended mode adds the harder contract: **zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run**.

**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt.
- **[unattended]** Run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and **zero compaction**. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate.

**Three pre-existing invariants (apply to BOTH modes)**: file-disjointness proof BEFORE spawn · 100% automatic merges · pre-spawn economics check.

**Two delivery layers**: L1 parallel `claude -p` worker spawns (primary lever for both modes) · L2 parallel tasks within one worker (weaker lever, when L1 isn't economic).

**Pre-reqs landed**: Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`) · Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8`/`f7de324`) · Q2b compact_marker frame + visualizer badge (`8abe4ef`) · Q3 turn→tool join fix (`8f4588b`) · optimization report generator (`b5edff2`) · adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`).

**Calibration corpus**: 525 turn rows (`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`) + 72 compaction events (`.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`) + per-CW report (`.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md`).

**Proposed domains (7)** — full scope in `.gsd-t/backlog.md` #14:
- D1 — Generic task-graph reader (parses domain tasks + cross-domain deps → DAG)
- D2 — `gsd-t parallel` CLI (subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level parallelism, `--mode` aware)
- D3 — Command-file integration (`execute`/`wave`/`quick`/`debug`/`integrate` consume task-graph)
- D4 — Dep-graph validation (refuses fan-out when deps unmet)
- D5 — File-disjointness prover (walks expected-touch lists, sequential fallback if unprovable)
- D6 — Pre-spawn economics + cost estimator (queries token-usage.jsonl for similar-task cost, mode-aware gating)
- D7 — Per-CW token attribution + compaction-event integration (`cw_id` tagging + estimator calibration loop)

**Success criteria**:
- [in-session] `gsd-t-execute` previously taking T min serially completes in ≤ T/2 min with N workers, zero pause/resume prompts
- [unattended] M1 → M5 minimum (ideally M1 → M10) runs end-to-end with **zero compaction events** during the autonomous chain

**Recommended flow**: `partition` → `discuss` (D1 task-graph schema + D6 estimator algorithm need design) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10.

**Previous milestone (M43)** completed 2026-04-21, v3.17.10 tagged. Reference DEFINED block preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/` archive.

**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.

**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.

**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).

**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.

**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.

**Out of scope (deferred)**:
- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.

**Contracts touched**:
- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)

**Success criteria**:
1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.

**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.

+- 2026-04-23 00:10: [execute M44 · unattended Wave 1 worker iter 2] Wave 1 closeout — D7 T4 test file (`test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js`, 19 tests) committed; D7 T5 doc-ripple already landed in-tree (requirements + architecture + progress log) now committed alongside; D1 T1-T5 and D7 T1-T5 task status flipped from `[ ] pending` to `[x] done (· commit `XXX`)` in both `tasks.md` files so the task-graph reader now reports them as satisfied deps for Wave 2. **Fix landed in same commit**: `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` top-level stdin wiring wrapped in `require.main === module` guard. Root cause: when the hook is `require()`d by a test file (as `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` does to exercise the `handle()` export directly), the module-level `process.stdin.setEncoding('utf8')` + `stdin.on('data'/'end')` listeners attach to the *test process's* stdin and keep the Node event loop alive indefinitely. All 19 D7 assertions pass in ~400ms isolated, but in the `npm test` full-suite run the file hangs on the unclosed stdin handle until the test runner kills it at its internal timeout (54885ms observed), which flagged the file as `✖` even though every sub-test was green. The `require.main === module` guard is the standard Node pattern for "only run top-level code when invoked as a script" and is functionally invisible in production (Claude Code invokes hooks via `node scripts/…` = `require.main === module` is true). After fix: 69/69 pass across D1 + D7 + m41-token-capture + compact-detector in 566ms combined. **Wave 1 gate status**: D1 DAG + D7 `cw_id` field + calibration hook all land; `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` new rows carry `cw_id` when supplied; `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` can now receive `compaction_post_spawn` calibration rows. Wave 2 (D4 + D5 + D6, all parallel-safe) unblocked.

On `payload.source === "compact"` it reads `.gsd-t/.unattended/state.json`, derives `spawn_id` (sessionId fallback chain), `cw_id` (= spawn_id for unattended workers), `task_id` (activeTask / task_id / currentTask fallback chain), `estimatedCwPct` (D6's pre-spawn prediction if recorded), `cwCeilingTokens` (defaults to 200000), then derives `actualCwPct` from `payload.input_tokens` (or nested `compactMetadata.preTokens`) divided by the ceiling, clamped to [0, 2.0]. Appends one `{type: "compaction_post_spawn", schemaVersion: 1, ts, cw_id, task_id, spawn_id, estimatedCwPct, actualCwPct}` row to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`. **Silent no-op** when payload isn't a compact source, when `.gsd-t/` is missing, when `state.json` is missing/unparseable/`status !== "running"`, when no `spawn_id` derivable, when no `input_tokens` derivable, or when `cwd` is non-absolute. Always exits 0 (throwing breaks Claude Code SessionStart). Zero ext deps, 1 MiB stdin cap, path-traversal guard mirroring the detector. **Coexistence verified by test**: pre-existing v1.0.0 detector rows (`source: "compact"`, no `type` field) remain intact when calibration row appended; consumers distinguish via `type` field (`"compaction_post_spawn"` vs absent). **Contracts bumped** in this domain (per constraints — must land in D7's commit, not Wave 2): `metrics-schema-contract.md` v2 → v2.1.0 (adds optional `cw_id` field with derivation rules — unattended: `cw_id == spawn_id`; in-session: `session_id + ":" + compaction_index` per the calibration hook's compact-fire counter), `compaction-events-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 (adds `compaction_post_spawn` event type, calibration hook lifecycle, guardrails, three-hook wiring matrix). **Test suite**: `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` — 19 tests, all green isolated: cw_id present → field serialized, cw_id numeric → coerced to string, cw_id absent/null/empty → field OMITTED (not null, not ""), captureSpawn forwards cw_id, `_buildJsonlRecord` cw_id branch matches session_id pattern, calibration hook with active spawn → calibration row written with correct task_id + estimatedCwPct + actualCwPct ~0.97 for 194000/200000, fallback to compactMetadata.preTokens, cwCeilingTokens override (95000/100000 = 0.95), no state.json → no row written, status !== running → no-op, malformed json → silent, source !== compact → no-op, missing input_tokens → no-op, empty/non-json stdin → no-op, non-absolute cwd → no-op, calibration row appends alongside v1.0.0 detector rows in same sink, zero ext deps. **Hard constraints all met** (per `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d7-per-cw-attribution/constraints.md`): backward-compatible cw_id (existing callers unchanged, absent ≠ null), silent no-op when no active spawn, zero ext deps, calibration event includes BOTH estimatedCwPct AND actualCwPct, contract bumps in D7's commit, `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` NOT modified. **Out of scope** (deferred): backfilling pre-D7 token-usage.jsonl rows with cw_id (525 historical rows; D6 falls back to per-iter median); economics logic (D6); parallel dispatch (D2); CW boundary detection algorithm beyond the spawn_id == cw_id rule. **D1 ran in parallel** (also Wave 1 Foundation per partition map: D1 + D7 both no-dep). [M44-D7-T4+T5] cw-attribution test + Wave 1 close-out + hook handle-leak fix

Closes M44 D7 (5/5) and marks M44 D1 (5/5) + D7 (5/5) tasks as
`[x] done` in both domain tasks.md files so the task-graph reader
reports them as satisfied deps for Wave 2.

- test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js (new, 19 tests) — covers cw_id
  pass-through (present / numeric coercion / absent / null / ""),
  captureSpawn forwarding, _buildJsonlRecord branch, calibration hook
  with active spawn, input_tokens fallback to compactMetadata.preTokens,
  cwCeilingTokens override, and 7 no-op paths (no state.json, non-running
  status, malformed json, non-compact source, missing input_tokens,
  empty stdin, non-absolute cwd), plus coexistence with v1.0.0 detector
  rows and zero-ext-deps invariant.

- scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js — wrap the module-level
  process.stdin.on('data'/'end') registration in
  `if (require.main === module) { … }`. Root cause: when the hook is
  require()'d by the D7 test file (to exercise the `handle()` export
  directly), stdin listeners attach to the *test* process and keep the
  Node event loop alive indefinitely — all 19 assertions pass in ~400ms
  isolated, but in the `npm test` full-suite run the test file gets
  killed at the runner's internal timeout (observed: 54.9s) and marked
  ✖ despite every sub-test being green. Standard Node pattern for
  "only run top-level code when invoked as a script"; functionally
  invisible in production since Claude Code invokes hooks via
  `node scripts/…` (require.main === module is true).

- docs/requirements.md — new §"M44 Per-CW Attribution (D7 — Wave 1
  foundation, 2026-04-22)" with 6 REQs (REQ-M44-D7-01..06) marked
  complete with evidence.
- docs/architecture.md — new §"Per-CW Attribution (M44 D7, v3.18.10+)"
  with producer/consumer diagram, cw_id derivation rules, calibration
  hook lifecycle, coexistence rules vs compact-detector, contract
  pointers, and test-file reference.
- .gsd-t/progress.md — Wave 1 closeout decision log entry documenting
  the commit chain (T1..T5 for both D1 and D7), the hook handle-leak
  root cause + fix, post-fix test counts (69/69 in 566ms), and Wave 2
  gate status (D4+D5+D6 unblocked).

Verification: `node --test test/m44-task-graph.test.js
test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js test/m44-compaction-detector.test.js
test/m41-token-capture.test.js` → 69/69 pass in 566ms. The pre-fix
full-suite 4 failures reduce to 3 (the D7 file-level timeout is gone);
the remaining 3 are unrelated pre-existing fails (event-stream entry
field, m43 progress.md stale-fixture milestone-complete check, watch
writer agent-id pattern) documented in prior commits.

Wave 1 gate met: D1 DAG emits cleanly + D7 cw_id field confirmed in
token-capture + calibration hook ready. Wave 2 (D4+D5+D6 — all
parallel-safe, read-only against DAG + token-usage.jsonl + scope.md)
unblocked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
[quick] transcript renderer — per-frame arrival timestamp pill

Adds a monospace HH:MM:SS pill (with ISO `title` tooltip) as the first
child of every frame div in the spawn transcript viewer so stuck-stream
detection becomes visual — two adjacent frames with the same HH:MM:SS
means nothing arrived between them; a long gap before a new timestamp
means the stream paused.

Single chokepoint: `appendFrame(el, arrivedAt)` now takes the arrival
Date as its second arg. SSE `onmessage` captures `arrivedAt = new
Date()` once per message and threads it through `renderFrame` → all
9 per-type render helpers (renderSystem, renderUserMessage,
renderAssistantText, renderThinking, renderToolUse, renderToolResult,
renderBoundary, renderRaw, renderCompactMarker).

CSS: new `.frame > .ts` rule with per-variant padding (.frame.tool-use,
.frame.thinking, .frame.tool-result, .frame.compact-marker) so the
pill aligns with the existing render layouts.

Tests: `test/m44-transcript-timestamp.test.js` — 7 asserts covering
CSS rule present, appendFrame signature, fmtTs/pad2 helpers, SSE
capture, renderFrame threading, script still syntactically valid,
and a sandboxed eval unit test of `fmtTs(Date)` → `"HH:MM:SS"`.
7/7 pass in 38ms.

User feedback that drove this during the M44 unattended run:
"Each message and tool call should include a time stamp so I can
easily see from the stream if something got stuck."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Worker iteration 2 complete. Summary:

**M44 Wave 1 closeout (commit `94a3bf6`)**
- D7-T4 test file `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` committed — 19 tests, all pass
- D7-T5 doc-ripple — new requirements §"M44 Per-CW Attribution" (6 REQs) + architecture §"Per-CW Attribution (M44 D7)" + progress log entry
- Task status flipped to `[x] done` for all 10 Wave 1 tasks (D1 T1-T5, D7 T1-T5)
- **Fix landed**: `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` top-level `process.stdin.on(…)` registration now guarded by `require.main === module`. The test file imports the hook's `handle()` export directly, which was attaching stdin listeners to the test process and keeping the Node event loop alive — causing a 54.9s test-runner timeout even though all 19 assertions pass. Standard Node pattern, production-invisible.

**Bonus (commit `f5e547a`)**
- Dangling transcript renderer timestamp-pill `[quick]` that was in-tree but uncommitted is now committed with its 7 tests.

**Wave 1 gate met**: D1 DAG emits cleanly; D7 `cw_id` field + calibration hook ready. Wave 2 (D4 depgraph validation + D5 file-disjointness prover + D6 pre-spawn economics — all parallel-safe, read-only against DAG) unblocked.

Post-fix isolated verification: **69/69 pass in 566ms** across D1 + D7 + m41-token-capture + compact-detector test files. The 4 pre-fix full-suite failures drop to 3 pre-existing unrelated fails.
